foundations.title: Colour System
foundations.content: The design system uses nine brand tokens drawn from the Figma colour palette. Each token has a specific role — do not use colour values that fall outside this set.

sections.title: Brand Colours
sections.body: Primary Navy (#1A2B4A) is the dominant brand colour used for buttons, active states, and filled UI elements. Accent Blue (#2B7DE9) is reserved for interactive elements such as links, focus rings, and highlighted selections. These two colours define the brand identity and should not be substituted.

sections.title: Surface Colours
sections.body: Background Grey (#F4F5F7) is used as the page or screen background. White (#FFFFFF) is used for cards, input fields, and overlay surfaces that sit above the background. Never use White as the page background — use Background Grey instead.

sections.title: Feedback Colours
sections.body: Success Green (#22A86B) communicates positive outcomes such as confirmed actions or valid inputs. Error Red (#D93636) communicates failures, blocking issues, and destructive actions. Use these colours only in their intended semantic roles — never use them for decorative or brand purposes.

sections.title: Text and Border Colours
sections.body: Text Primary (#1A1A2E) is used for all primary readable text including headings and labels. Text Muted (#6B7280) is used for secondary text, placeholders, and inactive states. Border (#E0E2E6) is used for all card borders, input field outlines, and separator lines.

foundations.title: Typography
foundations.content: The type system uses Inter exclusively across all four levels of the type ramp. Line height is set to Inter's natural intrinsic ratio of 1.21 at every size.

sections.title: Typeface
sections.body: Inter is the sole typeface used across the design system. It is used at four distinct sizes and weights forming a clear visual hierarchy: Heading (24px Bold), Subheading (18px Semi Bold), Body (14px Regular), and Caption (12px Regular). Do not introduce additional typefaces or weights outside this set.

sections.title: Heading — 24px Bold
sections.body: Used for page-level and primary section headings. Font weight 700. Line height 1.21. Paired with color-text-primary. One Heading per screen is recommended to preserve hierarchy.

sections.title: Subheading — 18px Semi Bold
sections.body: Used for card titles and secondary headings within a page. Font weight 600. Line height 1.21. Paired with color-text-primary. Use when content is nested beneath a Heading.

sections.title: Body — 14px Regular
sections.body: Used for all general reading content, descriptions, and input values. Font weight 400. Line height 1.21. Paired with either color-text-primary or color-text-muted depending on hierarchy. This is the default text size across the system.

sections.title: Caption — 12px Regular
sections.body: Used for navigation labels, metadata, timestamps, and secondary supporting text. Font weight 400. Line height 1.21. Typically paired with color-text-muted. Do not use Caption size for primary readable content.

foundations.title: Component Library
foundations.content: The component library contains four foundational components, each documented at a single default state in Figma. All components are built from the nine colour tokens and Inter type ramp.

sections.title: Design Approach
sections.body: Each component is designed at one representative state to establish the visual baseline. The Figma file documents the Button, Text Input, Card, and Bottom Nav Bar components. These form the core building blocks for screens and flows built on this design system.

sections.title: Colour Usage in Components
sections.body: All components draw exclusively from the nine brand colour tokens. Primary Navy is used for interactive and active elements such as buttons and the active bottom nav item. White and Border are the default surface and stroke combination for cards and inputs. Text Muted is used for all secondary text including placeholders, captions, and inactive nav labels.

sections.title: Spacing and Radius
sections.body: Components use consistent internal measurements derived from a 4px base unit. Cards and the Bottom Nav Bar use a 12px border radius. Buttons and Text Inputs use an 8px border radius. Internal padding for cards is 16px on all sides. Button horizontal padding is 20px. Input horizontal padding is 14px. Navigation bar padding is 16px horizontal and 12px vertical.
